stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates modern, efficient model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) point to its implementation within the standard ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic alleges Alibaba engaged in a massive data scraping/cloning attack against its Claude AI model.
The alleged attack involved using 25,000 accounts to generate over 28.8 million exchanges.
The incident raises significant concerns regarding AI model security, intellectual property, and data integrity in the LLM space.

stories.title: Apple to skip high-end M6 Mac chips in favor of AI-focused M7 line
stories.author: scrlk
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly bypassing high-end M6 Mac chips.
The company plans to introduce an AI-focused M7 line, including M7 Pro, Max, and Ultra variants.
This shift suggests a strategic pivot by Apple towards integrating advanced AI capabilities into its silicon roadmap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is reportedly delaying the release of its next major model, GPT-5.6.
The delay is allegedly due to a request from the Trump administration citing potential security concerns.
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman confirmed that GPT-5.6 will initially be released in a limited preview format.

stories.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Notion is integrating AI agents directly into its platform to manage user inboxes.
This move signals a shift away from traditional email interfaces toward agent-driven workflows.
The development is positioned as a response to the rise of AI agents replacing conventional email app usage.

stories.title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement
stories.author: Sambit Sahu
bullet_points: Introduces BINEVAL, a new framework to address the bottleneck of LLM evaluation.
Decomposes complex evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions for interpretability.
Aims to replace expensive human evaluation and opaque holistic scoring with debuggable, multi-dimensional metrics.

stories.title: TOPS: First-Principles Visual Token Pruning via Constructing Token Optimal Preservation Sets for Efficient MLLM Inference
stories.author: Shanghang Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational inefficiency of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) caused by a large number of visual tokens.
It introduces 'TOPS,' a novel visual token pruning method based on first-principles principles to improve inference efficiency.
TOPS aims to overcome limitations in existing pruning techniques, such as attention-based redundancy or instruction-agnostic diversity methods.

stories.title: OpenRCA 2.0: From Outcome Labels to Causal Process Supervision
stories.author: Pinjia He
bullet_points: Introduces OpenRCA 2.0, a new framework for testing LLM agentic capabilities through Root Cause Analysis (RCA).
Addresses a critical gap in existing RCA datasets by moving beyond simple root cause labeling to capture the full causal propagation path.
Presents PAVE, a novel step-wise labeling protocol that uses fault injection to provide rigorous, process-level supervision for LLM evaluation.

stories.title: HarmVideoBench: Benchmarking Harmful Video Understanding in Large Multimodal Models
stories.author: Guancheng Wan
bullet_points: Introduces HarmVideoBench, a new benchmark for evaluating harmful video understanding in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs).
Critiques existing benchmarks for oversimplifying harmful content into binary classification tasks, missing deep contextual harms.
Highlights the critical need for benchmarks to include explanatory rationales alongside classification results.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for users on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: YouTube updates Shorts to make it even more like TikTok
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: YouTube is integrating more TikTok-like features into its Shorts format.
A new 'clear screen' mode is being added to Shorts, removing on-screen icons and text.
The platform is replacing the traditional 'thumbs-up' button with a 'heart' icon for reactions.

stories.title: Instagram wants to monopolize your attention
stories.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
bullet_points: Instagram is rolling out new features for its smart TV app.
The primary goal of these features is to increase user engagement and time spent on the platform.
The updates are currently available on major TV platforms like Amazon Fire TV and Google TV.

stories.title: FCC may kill $2B program that connects schools and libraries to Internet
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is reportedly considering terminating a $2 billion program designed to provide internet access to schools and libraries.
The decision is linked to concerns raised by Carr regarding excessive student screen time.
The controversy frames the issue as a debate over government intervention in educational technology policy.

stories.title: Feds deny Polestar authorization to sell cars in US from model year 2027
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Federal authorities have denied Polestar authorization to sell its vehicles in the United States.
This denial means Polestar will not be able to officially sell its cars in the US market.
The decision contrasts with the situation for Volvo, which maintains authorization.

stories.title: New effort will get genome sequences for entire Endangered Species list
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Colossal Biosciences is undertaking a project to sequence genomes for all endangered species.
The initiative involves biobanking tissues from these endangered populations.
This represents a significant application of advanced genomics and conservation technology.

stories.title: Vulnerability of Natural Language Classifiers to Evolutionary Generated Adversarial Text
stories.author: Mohamed Elawady
bullet_points: The paper addresses the known vulnerability of deep learning NLP models to adversarial text inputs.
Adversarial attacks involve subtle, semantically similar token replacements designed to fool the models.
The research proposes 'GAversar' to counter these increasingly precise, model-specific attacks.

stories.title: Automating Potential-based Reward Shaping with Vision Language Model Guidance
stories.author: Daniel Kudenko
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in Reinforcement Learning (RL) by introducing automated reward shaping.
Proposes using Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to guide Potential-based Reward Shaping (PBRS), mitigating the need for manual heuristic design.
Aims to improve RL agent exploration and prevent reward hacking while preserving the optimal policy set.

stories.title: Safe Autoregressive Image Generation with Iterative Self-Improving Codebooks
stories.author: Jindong Gu
bullet_points: The research proposes a novel autoregressive approach for image generation, contrasting with continuous diffusion models.
This method uses discretized visual tokens derived from a codebook, allowing for sequential prediction.
The architecture supports unified multimodal models, enabling effective text conditioning for text-to-image tasks.

stories.title: Joint Learning of Experiential Rules and Policies for Large Language Model Agents
stories.author: Chao Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of effectively utilizing accumulated interaction experience in LLM agents within multi-step environments.
Compares two existing methods: using experience as external, interpretable natural-language rules, or using it for direct model parameter updates.
Proposes a joint learning framework to bridge the gap between interpretable rules and comprehensive policy improvement.

stories.title: Efficient foundation decoders for fault-tolerant quantum computing
stories.author: Yuxuan Du
bullet_points: The research addresses the scaling bottleneck in foundation decoders, which are crucial for fault-tolerant quantum computing.
Foundation decoders are high-capacity neural decoders used to handle errors in quantum systems.
A new framework, Neural Transfer Unification (NTU), is proposed to make these decoders more efficient at large code distances.

stories.title: Graph Neural Networks Applications Across Domains: All Insights You Need
stories.author: Abderaouf Bahi
bullet_points: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are becoming a standard model class for data with relational structures.
The research focus is shifting from 'if' message passing helps to 'where' the computational cost of graph structure is justified.
The survey provides a unified framework, deriving spectral and spatial GNN formulations from shared principles.

stories.title: Explaining Temporal Graph Neural Networks via Feature-induced Information Flow
stories.author: Shinichi Nakajima
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of explainability in complex Event-based Temporal Graph Neural Networks (ETGNNs).
ETGNNs are noted for strong performance in diverse applications like social network analysis and epidemic tracing.
The research aims to improve upon existing methods by providing a more comprehensive view of information flow within these models.

stories.title: Forecasting With LLMs: Improved Generalization Through Feature Steering
stories.author: Bradford Levy
bullet_points: The research investigates how Large Language Models (LLMs) perform forecasting by analyzing their internal states.
The study uses sparse autoencoders to distinguish between time-specific knowledge and generalizable patterns within LLMs.
Findings identify distinct features related to both time-aware and look-ahead-biased reasoning in LLMs.

stories.title: Automating Potential-based Reward Shaping with Vision Language Model Guidance
stories.author: Daniel Kudenko
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in Reinforcement Learning (RL) where agents lack intermediate feedback.
Proposes using Vision-Language Model (VLM) guidance to automate Potential-based Reward Shaping (PBRS).
Aims to mitigate reward hacking while preserving the optimal policy set, a known issue with naive reward shaping.

stories.title: RecallRisk-BERT: A Multi-Task Framework for Post-Report Medical Device Recall Triage
stories.author: Sevgi Yigit-Sert
bullet_points: Introduces RecallRisk-BERT, a multi-task framework designed to automate triage of medical device recall reports.
Addresses the challenge of managing the growing volume of FDA recall data by jointly modeling recall severity and root-cause categories.
Leverages advanced NLP (BERT) techniques for critical patient safety applications in the medical device industry.

stories.title: Learning to Fold: prizewinning solution at LeHome Challenge 2026 (1st place online, 2nd offline)
stories.author: Ilia Larchenko
bullet_points: The paper presents a solution to the LeHome Challenge 2026, focusing on bimanual garment folding using robotics.
The core technical contribution is an improved Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policy enhanced with a reinforcement learning loop.
A key innovation is using the policy network itself to predict success and progress, which drives advantage estimation.

stories.title: DMuon: Efficient Distributed Muon Training with Near-Adam Overhead
stories.author: Qian Wang
bullet_points: DMuon introduces an efficient distributed training method for matrix-orthogonalization-based optimizers (like Muon).
Matrix-aware updates offer a strong alternative to traditional element-wise optimization for large, complex models.
The research addresses a gap where current distributed training infrastructure is not optimized for matrix-level optimizers.

stories.title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement
stories.author: Sambit Sahu
bullet_points: Introduces BINEVAL, a new framework for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the limitations of current LLM evaluation methods (expensive human review, poor lexical metrics, opaque judges).
Decomposes complex evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions to produce interpretable, multi-dimensional scores.

stories.title: Paved with True Intents: Intent-Aware Training Improves LLM Safety Classification Across Training Regimes
stories.author: Yftah Ziser
bullet_points: Introduces AIMS, a new human-annotated dataset (1,724 prompts) designed to capture user intent in safety classification.
Proposes that safety classifiers should explicitly model user intent as a signal between the prompt and the final label.
Evaluates the effectiveness of intent-aware training across various LLM alignment techniques (SFT, RLHF, etc.).

stories.title: Forecasting With LLMs: Improved Generalization Through Feature Steering
stories.author: Bradford Levy
bullet_points: The research investigates how Large Language Models (LLMs) perform forecasting by examining their internal states.
The study uses sparse autoencoders to differentiate between time-specific knowledge and generalizable patterns within LLMs.
Researchers identified features related to both time-aware and look-ahead-biased reasoning in the LLMs.

stories.title: The Riddle Riddle: Testing Flexible Reasoning in Large Language Models and Humans
stories.author: Thomas L. Griffiths
bullet_points: The research introduces the 'riddle riddle paradigm' to test the nature of reasoning in LLMs.
It addresses the fundamental question of whether LLM performance stems from true flexible reasoning or mere pattern matching.
The paradigm uses word problems mimicking riddles, designed to require only literal interpretation for solving.

stories.title: NuclearQAv2: A Structured Benchmark for Evaluating Domain-Science Competence in Large Language Models
stories.author: Emily Taylor
bullet_points: Introduces NuclearQAv2, a new benchmark specifically designed to test Large Language Models (LLMs) in nuclear engineering.
Addresses the critical challenge of evaluating LLM reliability and competence in highly technical, domain-specific fields.
Focuses on testing not just factual knowledge, but also quantitative reasoning and conceptual understanding within nuclear science.

stories.title: Improving General Role-Playing Agents via Psychology-Grounded Reasoning and Role-Aware Policy Optimization
stories.author: Yabiao Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current role-playing agents (SFT) which only mimic behavior without deep reasoning.
Introduces Psy-CoT, a novel psychology-grounded Chain-of-Thought framework for better character portrayal.
Decomposes pre-response reasoning into three role-specific steps to improve out-of-distribution generalization.

stories.title: Just how sure are you? Improving Verbalized Uncertainty Calibration in Medical VQA
stories.author: Mark James Carman
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of overconfident predictions in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) when applied to Medical Visual Question Answering (VQA).
Highlights a gap in current research: existing confidence calibration methods are designed for text-only LLMs and fail to account for multimodal medical image understanding.
Proposes a novel training framework utilizing a composite loss function to improve the calibration of MLLMs in this specific domain.

stories.title: Lite-Harness SDK
stories.author: jeancarlosn
bullet_points: Introduction of a 'Lite-Harness SDK' designed to unify AI coding interfaces.
The SDK allows developers to easily swap between different AI coding harnesses (e.g., Claude Code, Codex).
This addresses the fragmentation in using various large language models for software development tasks.

stories.title: The agent-first approach to building products
stories.author: adamklein
bullet_points: The article discusses the growing trend of an 'agent-first' product development strategy.
It posits that future platform relevance hinges on the ability of AI agents to interact with them.
The content suggests a significant shift in how businesses must adapt to AI integration by 2026.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for competitive AI agent training.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition improves AI reasoning and generates better training data.
Focuses on the intersection of AI agent development and reinforcement learning through adversarial play.

stories.title: I Scanned 1,200 MCP Configs From GitHub. Here's What I Found.
stories.author: ad_0846
bullet_points: The article analyzes the security configuration of 1,200 real-world AI agent deployments found on GitHub.
It provides insights into the actual security posture of open-source AI agent implementations.
The author introduces an open-source tool used for this deep-dive security scanning.
